title: hieracium murorum subsp. symphacelodes
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. symphacelodes, also known as the Wall Hawkweed or Rough Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. symphacelodes include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and digestive issues.
